DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL THIRD-PARTY TO RESPOND TO A DOCUMENT SUBPOENA SERVED ON OCTOBER 30, 2018 IS UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. IF THE THIRD PARTY FAILS TO TIMELY RESPOND TO THE ORDER A MOTION FOR SANCTIONS MAY BE FILED FOR BOTH DISOBEDIENCE OF THE SUBPOENA AND THIS ORDER. ORDER IS SIGNED. Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Non-Party Dlw Construction Inc. To Produce Documents Responsive To Plaintiffs Subpoena Request For Monetary Sanctions Of 4,400.00
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jack Bollier, Esq., Rains Lucia Stern St. Phalle & Silver, P.C., 415-341-9341, appearing for Plaintiff John Williams;
No other appearance.

Party stipulates to the Judge Pro Tem.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS JOHN WILLIAMS, AIMEE WILLIAMS' Motion For An Order Compelling Non-Party Dlw Construction Inc. To Produce Documents Responsive To Plaintiffs Subpoena Request For Monetary Sanctions Of 4,400.00

Plaintiff's motion to compel third-party to respond to a document subpoena served on October 30, 2018 is unopposed and granted. DLW Construction, Inc. shall produce the documents identified in the third party subpoena within twenty days following both personal and mail service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. A copy of the subpoena with its proofs of service shall be attached to the Order as an exhibit. Because this motion to compel was brought more than one year after service of the subpoena the request for monetary sanctions is denied. If the third party fails to timely respond to the Order a motion for sanctions may be filed for both disobedience of the subpoena and this Order.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
